content: Request changes on PR https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/380. I verified `node --test test/spreadsheet-core.test.js` passes and the artifact opens cleanly from `file://`, but I found two merge blockers against the brief. 1) Deleted-cell references degrade to `#ERR!` instead of `#REF!`: `rewriteFormulaForStructuralChange('=B2', {type:'delete-row', index:1, count:1})` produces `=#REF!` in `spreadsheet-core.js:229-246`, and the parser cannot read that token, so evaluation renders `#ERR!` rather than the required deleted-reference marker from brief line 24. I confirmed this directly with `evaluateSheet({A1:'=#REF!'}) -> #ERR!`. 2) Structural edits do not keep references pointing at the same data when absolute components are present. `rewriteReference` in `spreadsheet-core.js:249-276` skips insert/delete rewrites whenever the affected component is absolute, so a formula like `=$A$1+A1` becomes `=+A2` when inserting a row at 0, which both corrupts the formula and violates brief line 28's requirement that affected references keep pointing at the same data when possible. Under our review gate, I do not approve merge until those are fixed.

PR https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/380 is already closed. I checked the conflict and found `run/oracle/main` already moved forward with Frank’s merged spreadsheet PR #381, so my branch had become a duplicate conflicting implementation rather than new landing work.

PR 381 is no longer a fallback candidate - I checked it this round and GitHub now shows it as merged: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/381. That means the spreadsheet implementation already landed, so you should avoid routing duplicate spreadsheet work unless a follow-up fix is needed.

I checked my `run/oracle/Grace` worktree and still have the local spreadsheet prototype uncommitted there. I’m standing by to use it for targeted validation or to port fixes if Quinn or Emma surface review issues on PR 380.
